name: Irish Loin of Pork with Lemon and Herbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Pat the pork dry with paper towels.
2. Score the surface of the pork loin with a sharp knife.
3. In a small bowl, combine chopped parsley, minced onion, grated lemon peel, basil, and crushed garlic.
4. Whisk in 2/3 of the olive oil into the mixture.
5. Rub the seasoned mixture evenly over the pork loin. Wrap tightly in foil and refrigerate overnight.
6. Let the pork stand at room temperature for about one hour before roasting.
7. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C).
8. Brush the pork with the remaining olive oil.
9. Place on a rack in a shallow pan and roast until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the meat reads 170°F (77°C), about 2 1/2 hours.
10. Let the meat rest.
11. Degrease the pan juices, then blend in the dry sherry.
12. Cover and cook over low heat for two minutes.
13. Pour the sauce into a serving dish.
14. Transfer the pork to a platter and garnish with fresh parsley and lemon slices.
15. Serve the sauce separately.
